title: The Third Sunday in Lent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## March 20, 2022 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
March 20, 2022 was the Third Sunday in Lent, falling within the Lenten season leading toward Easter (April 10, 2022). The RCL readings for this date would have included Exodus 17:1-7 (the striking of the rock at Meribah), Psalm 95, Romans 5:1-11, and John 4:5-42 (Jesus and the Samaritan woman at the well). The Gospel's themes of living water and spiritual thirst would have provided rich material for meditation during mid-Lent, a point of reflection before the final push toward Holy Week.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was in a period of significant internal discussion regarding LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological traditionalism, with the 2021 General Convention having recently affirmed expanded marriage and blessing rites. In the Colorado Diocese (Diocese of Colorado), Bishop Kimberly Lucas had been serving since 2019 and was navigating post-pandemic church reopening and attendance recovery. No major Episcopal or diocesan controversies appear to have peaked specifically in mid-March 2022.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver was emerging from winter weather patterns with spring conditions beginning. The Colorado legislature was in active session (typically runs into May), with education funding and housing affordability dominating local debates. Capitol Hill and downtown Denver were seeing continued recovery from pandemic-era business closures and discussing downtown revitalization. No specific major local crises or weather events marked the week immediately preceding March 20.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)
The dominant story was Russia's invasion of Ukraine (February 24 ongoing), with American attention focused on humanitarian crisis reports, NATO response, and weapons shipments to Ukrainian forces. Domestic politics centered on midterm election preparations, with Republicans gaining polling advantages and inflation becoming the central economic concern (gas prices near $4/gallon nationally). The Supreme Court confirmation hearings for Ketanji Brown Jackson were underway (March 21-23), generating significant media coverage and political intensity.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
The Ukraine war dominated the international landscape with Russian forces besieging major cities, particularly Kyiv and Mariupol, and reports of civilian casualties and possible war crimes emerging. China was conducting military exercises near Taiwan amid growing geopolitical tensions. North Korea had resumed missile testing after months of silence, conducting multiple launches in early March.

CULTURAL MOMENT
America was experiencing significant anxiety about inflation, global conflict, and the long-term impacts of the pandemic, with lingering restrictions being lifted (mask mandates ended in many states in March). The cultural mood was tense and divided; some celebrated a "return to normal" while others remained cautious about COVID variants. Sports remained a stable cultural touchstone (NBA and NCAA basketball heading toward playoffs), though much of the national conversation was consumed by the Ukraine crisis and its economic consequences.
